name: Cameroon
avoid: March to October is wetter across the coast and south, so intercity road journeys to Limbe and Kribi run slower and beach days are less reliable.
Carry some XAF cash for taxis, market stops, and attraction tickets because cards are inconsistent outside major hotels in Douala and Yaoundé.
Check current local conditions before committing to Bamenda or longer Northwest road legs, as transport schedules and checkpoints can change faster there than on the coast.
stopoverNote: Douala is the easiest international entry and pairs naturally with Limbe first; save the longer Amour Mezam and Touristique Express coach legs for once you are settled, and treat the Camair-Co/Waza safari idea as a separate Yaoundé add-on rather than forcing it into the core coastal loop.

cities.name: Douala
cities.notes: La Nouvelle Liberté, Doual'Art, cathedral-quarter stops, Parcours Vita, and the country's busiest hotel and restaurant scene.

cities.name: Yaoundé
cities.notes: National Museum, Monument de la Réunification, Mefou primate day trips, and the cleanest onward bus and domestic-flight logistics.

cities.name: Limbe
cities.notes: Black-sand beach atmosphere, Limbe Wildlife Centre, botanical gardens, and quick taxi access to Buea for Mount Cameroon trailheads.

cities.name: Kribi
cities.notes: Lobé Falls canoe rides, long beach stretches, seafood restaurants, and easy slow days before returning to Douala.

cities.name: Bamenda
cities.notes: Mankon heritage, market life, highland viewpoints, and an optional long heritage detour toward Foumban.

itinerary.sim: Orange Cameroun and MTN Cameroon are the easiest choices for this route; budget roughly ₹560–₹1,680 for a starter data bundle that covers maps, messaging, and hotel bookings across Douala, Yaoundé, Limbe, and Kribi, with patchier speeds on longer Northwest road sections.

days.theme: Bonanjo landmarks, public art, and a soft landing in Cameroon’s busiest gateway city

activities.name: Airport taxi into Akwa, Bonanjo, or Bonapriso
La Nouvelle Liberté and Doual'Art stop
Cathedral of Saints Peter and Paul Bonadibong visit
Taxi hops to Parcours Vita or Marché Central

days.theme: Wildlife rescue, botanical gardens, and Limbe’s laid-back black-sand coast

activities.name: Shared taxi or hired car from Douala to Limbe
Limbe Wildlife Centre international adult entry
Limbe Botanic Garden visit
Short taxi rides between the wildlife centre, gardens, and Down Beach

days.theme: Buea slopes and the classic Mount Cameroon add-on from the coast

activities.name: Taxi from Limbe to Buea trailhead and back
Mount Cameroon National Park foreign hiker permit
Guide or porter arrangement at the Buea office
Down Beach sunset or seafood dinner back in Limbe

days.theme: Capital museums, reunion landmarks, and practical city logistics

activities.name: Shared taxi or coach from Limbe into Yaoundé
Monument de la Réunification and city viewpoint loop
National Museum of Cameroon visit
City taxi rides between Centre Ville, the museum district, and Mvolyé

days.theme: Wildlife or Far North planning day, with the option to bolt on Cameroon’s big safari detour

activities.name: Mefou National Park transport and sanctuary visit from Yaoundé
Camair-Co Yaoundé-Maroua return if you add the Waza safari extension
Waza National Park foreign visitor entry for the add-on safari day
Touristique Express seat hold or next-leg ticket planning toward Kribi

days.theme: Market energy, Mankon heritage, and a first look at the Northwest highlands

activities.name: Amour Mezam coach from Yaoundé to Bamenda
Mankon Fon’s Palace visit and local guide donation
Bamenda Main Market walk and short taxi hops
Sunset highland viewpoint drive around town

days.theme: Optional west-highlands heritage day with the long Foumban palace detour

activities.name: Private-car day trip from Bamenda toward Foumban and back
Foumban Royal Palace and museum foreign visitor ticket
Ring Road photo stops on the return to Bamenda
Evening taxi back into central Bamenda

days.theme: Atlantic beach time and the signature Lobé Falls pirogue ride

activities.name: Return via Yaoundé and continue by Touristique Express or shared taxi to Kribi
Lobé Falls canoe trip
Taxi hops between town beach, Lobé, and the seafood strip
Kribi beach evening and waterfront walk

days.theme: Slow beach recovery day before the final return to Douala

activities.name: Morning beach time or short pirogue hire near Lobé
Sea turtle coast or village-side excursion toward Ebodjé
Shared taxi or intercity bus back to Douala
Short town taxi rides for markets or the return station

connections.method: Shared taxis and hired cars are the easy coastal move from Douala, with Buea used as the inland pivot if you plan to hike Mount Cameroon first.
Road back via Buea and Douala is the standard transfer; leave early because city traffic around Douala can add real time to what looks short on the map.
Amour Mezam is the most recognizable coach operator on this leg; daytime departures are easier if you want more flexibility on arrival than an overnight ride.
This is best treated as a split transfer back through Yaoundé, then onward by Touristique Express or shared taxi to the coast, rather than trying to improvise it as one long same-day scramble.
Shared taxis and intercity buses are frequent enough for the return to Douala, making Kribi the simplest beach finish before an international departure.

extras: November to February is the sweet spot for this route: the Douala-Limbe-Kribi coast is drier, road delays ease up, and beach days are more dependable than in the heavier rains.
Keep small XAF notes ready for taxi bargaining and attraction entries, because even good hotels often cannot smooth out everyday cash needs once you are outside their compound.
Mount Cameroon normally works better as a Buea-based guided outing from Limbe than as a rushed same-day summit attempt; give yourself flexibility if weather closes in.
The Waza safari idea is real but logistically separate from the core south-and-west loop, so only add the Camair-Co Maroua detour if you have extra days beyond the main eight-day plan.
For Bamenda, check the latest on-ground conditions and favor trusted hotel transport or pre-arranged cars for early or late arrivals rather than winging intercity transfers on the spot.

Douala: Cathedral of Saints Peter and Paul Douala
